result:
I'd make people *actually* curious again.

Not the shallow, scroll-and-forget kind. The real kind—the kind that makes you sit with questions that don't have easy answers, that makes you uncomfortable, that forces you to update your beliefs when the evidence shows up. The kind that treats "I don't know" as an invitation instead of a threat.

Most of the world's worst problems seem to come from the same root: people deciding they already understand everything that matters and then building their lives, politics, relationships, and institutions around that assumption. If genuine curiosity became the default setting instead of an occasional hobby, a lot of the rest would sort itself out over time. Not perfectly. Not quickly. But fundamentally.

message.reasoning_content: If I could wave a magic wand and alter the world in just one way, I'd ensure that every single person had the same insatiable curiosity about the universe that drives my existence.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
